sourceMovie.title: Falling Down
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Falling Down for the same reason it broke through in 1993: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Joel Schumacher keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Drive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Falling Down clicked for you, Drive (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Falling Down?
faq.answer: Try Drive, Gangster Squad, Shot Caller—each shares DNA with Falling Down while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Falling Down?
faq.answer: Falling Down (1993) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Falling Down worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
